title: Handle Hospital Exceptions Caused By Tipper Tote Detection Or Clamp Failures
summary: Reference guidance from the training source for recognizing tipper exception conditions that route an item or AGB to the hospital, and for monitoring the hospital area for those exceptions. The source identifies tote visibility problems at the photo sensors and inability to clamp fully as example causes, and states that the hospital is typically monitored by a supervisor or nearby tipper positions rather than a dedicated operator.
when_to_use: Use this reference when observing tipper operations or hospital flow and you need to recognize source-described exception patterns that cause an item or AGB to be sent to the hospital, and to understand how the hospital area is typically monitored.
not_for: Do not use this as a corrective procedure for resolving hospital items after arrival, because the source does not provide detailed hospital handling steps.
Do not use this as authority for maintenance, controls changes, AGV removal, or recovery actions not described in this source segment.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe tote position, sensor visibility, clamp behavior, and hospital arrivals as described in the source.
Supervisor or nearby tipper positions may monitor the hospital area and respond to exceptions, as described by the source.

safety_notes: This source segment is observational/reference guidance only and does not provide physical intervention steps.
Stop or escalate if handling the hospital item would require actions not described in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to the tipper tote position
Ability to observe photo-sensor-related tote visibility at the tipper
Ability to observe clamp or grip behavior
Access to or visibility of the hospital area
Audible awareness of the hospital alert mentioned in the source

access_required: Ability to observe the hospital area
system_preconditions: Tipper operation is active or being observed in a context where tote detection and clamp behavior can be seen
Hospital area is available for monitoring

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing the modular layout including the hospital area, with nearby transcript describing tipper exception examples and hospital monitoring coverage.

steps.title: Check whether the tote is visible to the photo sensors
steps.purpose: Identify one of the source-described exception causes that can route an item or AGB to the hospital.
steps.instruction: At the tipper, observe whether the tote is positioned so the photo sensors can see it correctly. Use the source examples of the tote being pushed back too far or being on its side as indicators that the tote may not be seen correctly.
steps.expected_result: The observer can determine whether the tote appears correctly positioned for sensor detection or matches a source-described misposition condition.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote appears positioned so the photo sensors can see it correctly.
steps.failure_condition: The tote is not seen correctly by the photo sensors, including source-described examples such as being pushed back too far or being on its side.

actions.label: Observe tote position at tipper
actions.exact_action: Identify whether the tote is positioned so the photo sensors can see it correctly; examples given are the tote being pushed back too far or being on its side.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital area in the layout and the nearby transcript context describing tote visibility failures at the tipper.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote visibility problem requires corrective handling steps not described in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If photo sensors do not see the tote correctly, or if the system cannot clamp down fully, it sends the item to the hospital.

Nearby transcript explains operational failure examples at a tipper, such as photo sensors not seeing a tote, tote misalignment, or inability to clamp fully, which causes the item or AGV to be sent to the hospital.

steps.title: Observe whether the tipper can clamp the tote fully
steps.purpose: Identify the second source-described exception cause that can route an item or AGB to the hospital.
steps.instruction: Observe whether the tipper can clamp or grip the tote fully during the tipper process.
steps.expected_result: The observer can determine whether the clamp fully engages the tote or whether the source-described clamp failure condition is present.
steps.healthy_condition: The tipper clamps or grips the tote fully.
steps.failure_condition: The system cannot clamp down fully on the tote.

actions.label: Observe clamp engagement
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the tipper can clamp or grip the tote fully.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Transcript-supported context that inability to clamp fully is a hospital-routing exception.

stop_or_escalate_if: Clamp failure requires corrective or maintenance actions not described in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If photo sensors do not see the tote correctly, or if the system cannot clamp down fully, it sends the item to the hospital.

steps.title: Recognize hospital routing when detection or clamp failure occurs
steps.purpose: Connect the observed exception condition to the source-described system outcome.
steps.instruction: If the source-described condition is that the system cannot see the tote correctly or cannot clamp down fully, recognize that the item or AGB is sent to the hospital.
steps.expected_result: The observer understands that the documented exception outcome is hospital routing.
steps.healthy_condition: No source-described detection or clamp failure is present, so hospital routing is not indicated by this reference.
steps.failure_condition: A source-described detection or clamp failure is present and the item or AGB is routed to the hospital.

actions.label: Identify hospital-routing condition
actions.exact_action: Recognize that if the system cannot see the tote correctly or cannot clamp down fully, it sends the item or AGB to the hospital.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital area shown in the layout and transcript context linking tipper exceptions to hospital routing.

stop_or_escalate_if: The situation requires a recovery or corrective workflow not provided in this source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If photo sensors do not see the tote correctly, or if the system cannot clamp down fully, it sends the item to the hospital.
Hospital is an exception-handling area in OptiSweep training. AGBs may be sent there when photo sensors cannot see a tote correctly or when the mechanism cannot clamp down fully.

steps.title: Monitor the hospital area for arriving exceptions
steps.purpose: Ensure source-described hospital exceptions are noticed for handling coverage.
steps.instruction: Monitor the hospital area for arriving exceptions, noting that the source says it is usually watched by a supervisor or nearby tipper positions rather than continuously manned.
steps.expected_result: Hospital arrivals are noticed by shared coverage rather than a dedicated station operator.
steps.healthy_condition: The hospital area is being watched by the supervisor or nearby tipper positions as described in the source.
steps.failure_condition: Hospital exceptions arrive without being noticed or coverage is unclear.

actions.label: Watch hospital area
actions.exact_action: Monitor the hospital area for arriving exceptions.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital area location in the layout and transcript context stating the hospital is not a continuously manned station.

stop_or_escalate_if: No one is available to monitor or respond to hospital items as described in the source.
Handling the hospital item requires steps not described in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The hospital is not a manned station; the supervisor bounces back and forth and nearby tipper positions may watch it.
Hospital station staffing is described as shared coverage rather than a dedicated operator. Supervisors and nearby tipper positions monitor the hospital area.

steps.title: Use the audible indication to notice hospital items
steps.purpose: Use the source-mentioned audible cue to help detect hospital arrivals needing attention.
steps.instruction: Use the audible indication mentioned in the source to help notice hospital items that need attention, if present.
steps.expected_result: Audible indication helps draw attention to hospital exceptions.
steps.healthy_condition: An audible cue is present and helps alert nearby personnel to hospital items.
steps.failure_condition: No audible cue is heard when expected, or the item still requires attention beyond what the source describes.

actions.label: Listen for hospital alert
actions.exact_action: Use the audible indication mentioned in the source to help notice hospital items that need attention, if present.

stop_or_escalate_if: The audible indication is absent or insufficient and the item requires handling steps not described in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Supervisors and nearby tipper positions monitor the hospital area, and an audible noise helps draw attention.

success_criteria: Source-described tipper exception conditions are recognized correctly.
The observer understands that tote visibility failures or inability to clamp fully route the item or AGB to the hospital.
The hospital area is monitored in accordance with the source-described shared coverage model.
healthy_conditions: The tote is positioned so the photo sensors can see it correctly.
The tipper can clamp or grip the tote fully.
Hospital monitoring coverage is present through the supervisor or nearby tipper positions.
Audible indication, if present, helps draw attention to hospital items.
failure_conditions: Photo sensors do not see the tote correctly.
The tote is pushed back too far or on its side, as described in the source examples.
The system cannot clamp down fully.
A hospital item requires corrective handling steps not provided by this source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate or stop if handling the hospital item would require steps not described in the source.
Escalate if the exception requires corrective, maintenance, or recovery actions beyond recognizing and monitoring the condition.
Escalate if hospital monitoring coverage is not available as described by the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Facility layout including the hospital area, with transcript context describing tipper detection/clamp failures and hospital monitoring.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: If photo sensors do not see the tote correctly, or if the system cannot clamp down fully, it sends the item to the hospital.
The hospital is not a manned station; the supervisor bounces back and forth and nearby tipper positions may watch it.
